items.title: Why Comfort Makes You Restless
items.prompt: Create a cinematic 45-second documentary about why a life built only around comfort can create boredom, restlessness, and loss of meaning. Use a consistent protagonist, repetitive spaces, symbolic routine, natural lighting, short captions, and end with the value of meaningful difficulty.
reasons: The framing creates a clear curiosity gap.
The topic strongly matches Mind Frontier's psychology audience.
The topic fits the channel's philosophy positioning.

items.title: The Psychology of People Pleasing
items.prompt: Create a cinematic 45-second documentary about how people pleasing begins as a survival strategy and slowly erases personal identity. Use a consistent protagonist, mirrors, crowded rooms, quiet aftermath, reflective narration, short captions, and end with a boundary-focused insight.
reasons: The framing creates a clear curiosity gap.
The topic strongly matches Mind Frontier's psychology audience.
The prompt is dense and may reduce creative focus.

items.title: Why Your Brain Avoids Discomfort
items.prompt: Create a cinematic 45-second documentary about why the brain prefers familiar discomfort over uncertain growth. Use a consistent protagonist, repeated routines, thresholds, doors, shoes, short captions, and end with a small-action approach to change.
reasons: The framing creates a clear curiosity gap.
The topic strongly matches Mind Frontier's psychology audience.
